title: Envoy Proxy Endpoint
description: An Envoy Proxy Endpoint represents a single upstream host within a cluster that can receive proxied traffic. Endpoints are grouped into localities for zone-aware routing and are assigned load balancing weights, health status, and priority levels. The Endpoint Discovery Service (EDS) dynamically provides endpoint assignments to Envoy, enabling real-time updates as upstream hosts are added, removed, or change health state.

cluster_name.description: The name of the cluster that these endpoints belong to.

endpoints.description: A list of endpoint groups organized by locality.

locality.description: The locality associated with this set of endpoints, used for zone-aware routing.

region.description: The geographic region, such as us-east-1.

zone.description: The availability zone within the region, such as us-east-1a.

sub_zone.description: A further subdivision within the zone.

lb_endpoints.description: The list of individual endpoints in this locality.

address.description: The network address of the endpoint.
The IP address or hostname of the upstream host.

port_value.description: The port number of the upstream host.

protocol.description: The protocol for this socket address.

mode.description: The mode for the Unix domain socket.

health_check_config.description: Optional health check configuration overrides for this endpoint.

port_value.description: An alternate port to use for health checking this endpoint.

hostname.description: An alternate hostname to use for health checking.
The hostname of the endpoint, used for SNI matching and auto-host-rewrite.

health_status.description: The health status of the endpoint as determined by the management server or health checker.

metadata.description: Opaque metadata associated with the endpoint for use in load balancing and routing.

load_balancing_weight.description: The load balancing weight of this endpoint. Endpoints with higher weights receive proportionally more traffic.
The load balancing weight for this entire locality. Used for inter-locality traffic distribution.

priority.description: The priority of this locality. Envoy will route to higher priority localities first, falling back to lower priority when higher priority localities become unavailable.

named_endpoints.description: A map of named endpoints that can be referenced by name in lb_endpoints.

policy.description: Load balancing policy settings for the endpoint group.

overprovisioning_factor.description: Priority-level overprovisioning factor used to determine when to spread traffic to lower priority levels. Defaults to 140.

endpoint_stale_after.description: Duration after which endpoints are considered stale if not refreshed via EDS.

drop_overloads.description: Configuration for dropping a percentage of traffic.

drop_percentage.description: The percentage of traffic to drop.
